title: Rhode Island stroke survivor relearns to speak practicing his Dunkin’ order
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Rich McMahon, a Rhode Island stroke survivor, regains speech by practicing his Dunkin' coffee order: large iced coffee, two creams, two sugars.
A carotid artery dissection led to two strokes for McMahon, originally diagnosed as an ocular migraine at Kent County Hospital.
Liz McMahon shared Rich's progress on TikTok, gaining supportive responses from speech therapists and stroke survivors worldwide.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander wins superstar battle vs. Jaylen Brown as Thunder beat Celtics: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 35 points leading the Thunder to a 104-102 victory over the Celtics.
Gilgeous-Alexander set a record for scoring 20 points in 127 consecutive games, surpassing Wilt Chamberlain.
Jaylen Brown scored 34 points for the Celtics, who struggled without Jayson Tatum and Derrick White.

title: Patriots’ riskiest free-agent signing hopes injuries are in the past
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alijah Vera-Tucker joins the Patriots on a three-year, $42 million contract with $17 million guaranteed.
Vera-Tucker missed the entire 2025 season due to a torn triceps, marking his third season-ending injury in five years.
The contract includes $4.25 million per-season bonuses tied to Vera-Tucker's game-day activations.

title: NH mom running Boston Marathon for Spaulding after son’s friend injured at beach
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jessica O'Brien from Milford, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
O'Brien's motivation stems from Aiden Sloan, her son's friend, who was seriously injured last summer at Hampton Beach.
Funds raised by O'Brien will support Spaulding's care programs, which played a critical role in Sloan's recovery.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test readers' knowledge of recent Greater Boston news stories.
The quiz covers trending topics and current events in the Greater Boston area.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor, oversees the news quiz segment.

title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary William Galvin agreed to issue overdue reports on jail voting after a lawsuit by Lawyers for Civil Rights.
The VOTES Act requires reports on incarcerated voter participation, with Galvin's office failing to comply for two state elections.
Galvin's office attributed report delays to technical issues and aims to improve data collection before upcoming primaries.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam signed a three-year contract with the New England Patriots after six years with the Buffalo Bills.
Gilliam likens new quarterback Drake Maye to former NFL MVP Josh Allen, citing similar physical and playmaking abilities.
As a fullback, Gilliam aims to bolster the Patriots' run game, helping facilitate success for running backs Stevenson and Henderson.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez underwent shoulder surgery and will miss at least the first two months of the 2026 season.
Gonzalez was a key player in 2025 with a .305 average, often filling a platoon role against left-handed pitching.
Boston Red Sox signed Danny Coulombe, a veteran lefty reliever, to a one-year contract to strengthen the bullpen.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, a high school senior from Lynn, charged with the murder of Janet Swallow, 68, in Danvers.
Lynn police found DeMayo with a knife stained with what appeared to be blood, over seven miles from the crime scene.
Essex County DA Paul Tucker described the attack as random with no known connection between DeMayo and Swallow.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are seeking a suspect who allegedly assaulted a pregnant woman at a Roxbury laundromat on March 1.
The incident began as a payment dispute and escalated when the suspect allegedly kicked the victim in the stomach.
Police describe the suspect as a 6’0” light-skinned male wearing a Yankees hat, beige hoodie, and blue pants.
